scope.summary:
Goal: Implement the four GO-menu navigation behaviors for the native code editor: (1) Go to Next Diagnostic (F8), (2) Go to Previous Diagnostic (Shift+F8), (3) Navigate Back (Alt+Left), (4) Navigate Forward (Alt+Right). Items (1)/(2) traverse the ordered diagnostic markers produced by MT-007's gutter (errors/warnings/info pushed from the MT-008 LSP client via push_diagnostics) with wraparound at the ends. Items (3)/(4) walk an editor jump-history stack that records cursor positions across files whenever the user performs a navigation jump (go-to-definition, find-references result open, symbol/outline jump, and goto-line). This MT also wires the four items into the GO menu (the editor menu bar built in the WP-011 shell, reusing top_menu_bar.rs and pane_registry.rs) and adds the matching CodeEditorAction enum variants into the canonical keymap owned by MT-010.

Files to CREATE:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/navigation.rs` — the diagnostic-traversal logic. Define `pub struct DiagnosticNavigator;` (stateless; it reads live state from the gutter/diagnostic store). Expose `pub fn next_diagnostic(markers: &[DiagnosticMarker], cursor: BufferPosition) -> Option<BufferPosition>` and `pub fn prev_diagnostic(markers: &[DiagnosticMarker], cursor: BufferPosition) -> Option<BufferPosition>`. `DiagnosticMarker` is the marker type already defined by MT-007 (re-use it; do NOT redefine it — import via `handshake_native::code_editor::gutter::DiagnosticMarker`). `BufferPosition { line: usize, column: usize }` is the editor's existing cursor type (re-use the type MT-001/MT-003 established for the cursor; if a shared `Position`/`Caret` type exists, import it rather than create a parallel one). Ordering rule: markers are sorted by (line, column) ascending. `next_diagnostic` returns the first marker strictly after the cursor; if none, wrap to the first marker (index 0). `prev_diagnostic` returns the last marker strictly before the cursor; if none, wrap to the last marker. Return `None` only when the marker list is empty.
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/jump_history.rs` — the back/forward jump stack. Define `pub struct JumpEntry { pub file_path: PathBuf, pub position: BufferPosition }` and `pub struct JumpHistory { entries: Vec<JumpEntry>, cursor: usize }`. Expose: `JumpHistory::new() -> Self`, `record(&mut self, entry: JumpEntry)` (push: if the user previously navigated back and then makes a NEW jump, truncate the forward tail before pushing — VS Code semantics; coalesce consecutive entries that point at the same file+position within a small line tolerance so trivial cursor moves do not flood the stack), `back(&mut self, current: JumpEntry) -> Option<JumpEntry>` (move the cursor one entry toward the past and return the prior location, recording `current` so Forward can return to it), `forward(&mut self) -> Option<JumpEntry>` (move toward the future), `can_back(&self) -> bool`, `can_forward(&self) -> bool`. The stack must be cross-file: each entry carries its own `file_path` so Navigate Back can restore a position in a different file than the one currently focused. Cap the stack at a fixed size (e.g. 50 entries) by dropping the oldest.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/keymap.rs` (owned by MT-010 — extend, do NOT rewrite its existing dispatch table) — add four `CodeEditorAction` enum variants: `GoToNextDiagnostic`, `GoToPrevDiagnostic`, `NavigateBack`, `NavigateForward`. Register default bindings in the canonical keymap table: F8 -> GoToNextDiagnostic, Shift+F8 -> GoToPrevDiagnostic, Alt+ArrowLeft -> NavigateBack, Alt+ArrowRight -> NavigateForward, using `egui::KeyboardShortcut { modifiers, key }` consistent with the existing MT-010 entries. Wire the dispatch arms: GoToNextDiagnostic/GoToPrevDiagnostic call `navigation::next_diagnostic`/`prev_diagnostic` with the current marker list + cursor and, on `Some(pos)`, move the editor caret to `pos` AND push the pre-jump location into the JumpHistory; NavigateBack/NavigateForward call `JumpHistory::back`/`forward` and, on `Some(entry)`, open `entry.file_path` (if different from the active file) and move the caret to `entry.position`.

GO-menu wiring: the editor menu bar lives in the WP-011 shell (top_menu_bar.rs). Add four GO-menu items (labels: "Go to Next Problem", "Go to Previous Problem", "Back", "Forward") that each dispatch the matching CodeEditorAction through the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), so menu click and keybinding share one code path. Reuse the WP-011 pane registry (pane_registry.rs) to resolve the currently focused code-editor pane, and reuse the WP-011 command bus rather than calling editor methods directly. Forward/Back menu items should be disabled (greyed) when `can_forward()`/`can_back()` are false.

React reference: `app/src/components/operator/ProblemsView.tsx` — port its diagnostic ORDERING only (how problems are sorted by file then line then column then severity). The native ordering in `navigation.rs` must match ProblemsView so that F8 traversal order equals the Problems-list order a user would see. Do not port React rendering; ordering semantics only.

Backend API binding: NONE. This MT is pure frontend. Diagnostic markers come from MT-007's in-process gutter store (which MT-008 already populated from the LSP client). Jump positions come from in-process editor cursor state. No new handshake_core API call is introduced. (PostgreSQL/EventLedger is irrelevant here — no persistence; the jump stack is in-memory session state.)

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Confirm the MT-007 `DiagnosticMarker` type and the editor's cursor `BufferPosition` type names by reading `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/gutter.rs` and the cursor module; import them — never redefine.
2. Implement `navigation.rs` pure functions first; they are trivially unit-testable without egui.
3. Implement `jump_history.rs` with VS Code back/forward truncation semantics; unit-test push/back/forward and the cross-file path retention.
4. Extend `keymap.rs`: add the four variants, the four default shortcuts, and the four dispatch arms. Keep the existing MT-010 entries intact.
5. Add the four GO-menu items in the WP-011 shell menu bar (top_menu_bar.rs), each dispatching via the command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs); gate Back/Forward enabled-state on can_back/can_forward.
6. AccessKit: give each GO-menu item a stable AccessKit author_id reusing the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) — `menu-go-next-diagnostic`, `menu-go-prev-diagnostic`, `menu-go-back`, `menu-go-forward`, role=MenuItem — so swarm agents can invoke navigation deterministically.
7. When a goto-def / find-references / outline-jump / goto-line jump occurs (MT-006, MT-008 dispatch sites), call `JumpHistory::record` with the PRE-jump location so Navigate Back can return there. This means MT-006/MT-008 dispatch arms must call into the shared JumpHistory; add the record() call at those dispatch sites in keymap.rs without altering their existing jump behavior.

Dependencies / unblock conditions:
- MT-006 (goto-line) merged — provides one jump source and the editor caret-move primitive.
- MT-007 (gutter diagnostics) merged — provides the `DiagnosticMarker` list traversed by F8/Shift+F8.
- MT-008 (LSP client) merged — provides goto-def / find-references jump sources and the diagnostics feed.
- MT-010 (unified keymap) merged — provides the `CodeEditorAction` enum and dispatch table this MT extends.
- Cross-WP: WP-011 (native shell) provides the menu bar (top_menu_bar.rs), pane registry (pane_registry.rs), command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs), and AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) reused for GO-menu wiring; these are already-shipped WP-011 modules under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/.

Risk if missed: the four GO menu items are dead/non-functional; diagnostic traversal (F8/Shift+F8) and back/forward navigation (Alt+Left/Right) parity with VS Code is unmet, and the MT-044 Parity Proof Suite cannot prove the navigation feature row.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `next_diagnostic` returns the first marker strictly after the cursor, and wraps to the first marker when the cursor is at or past the last marker — proven by unit test in navigation.rs covering: cursor before all markers, cursor between two markers, cursor at the last marker (wrap), and empty marker list (returns None).
AC-002: `prev_diagnostic` returns the last marker strictly before the cursor, and wraps to the last marker when the cursor is at or before the first marker — proven by the symmetric unit test.

AC-003: JumpHistory push/back/forward semantics match VS Code: after a goto-def jump, Navigate Back restores the prior file_path + BufferPosition; a NEW jump after a Back truncates the forward tail — proven by unit test in jump_history.rs.

AC-004: Cross-file jump history retains the source file path: recording a jump from file A to file B and then Navigate Back returns an entry whose file_path == A (not the currently focused file) — proven by unit test.

AC-005: The four CodeEditorAction variants (GoToNextDiagnostic, GoToPrevDiagnostic, NavigateBack, NavigateForward) exist in keymap.rs with default bindings F8 / Shift+F8 / Alt+ArrowLeft / Alt+ArrowRight, without altering the pre-existing MT-010 keymap entries — proven by grep of the keymap table and a cargo test asserting the four shortcuts resolve to the four actions.

AC-006: egui_kittest harness: with a buffer containing diagnostic markers on known lines, pressing F8 moves the caret to the next diagnostic line; pressing it again advances; pressing it at the last marker wraps to the first — proven by kittest caret-position assertions.

AC-007: The four GO-menu items are present and dispatch via the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs, not direct editor method calls); Back/Forward items are disabled when can_back()/can_forward() are false — proven by kittest menu interaction + AccessKit MenuItem nodes menu-go-next-diagnostic / menu-go-prev-diagnostic / menu-go-back / menu-go-forward.
AC-008: `cargo test -p handshake-native` passes with all navigation and jump_history tests green; no panics.
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native next_diagnostic` => moves to the next marker by position and wraps at end; all GREEN.
PT-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native prev_diagnostic` => moves to the previous marker by position and wraps at start; all GREEN.
PT-003: `cargo test -p handshake-native jump_history` => goto-def then Navigate Back restores prior file+position; new jump truncates forward tail; all GREEN.
PT-004: `cargo test -p handshake-native jump_history_cross_file` => cross-file jump history retains the source file path; GREEN.
PT-005: egui_kittest test `code_editor_f8_next_diagnostic` => F8 moves the cursor to the next diagnostic line; assertion on caret line index; GREEN.
PT-006: AccessKit tree dump from kittest contains MenuItem nodes menu-go-next-diagnostic, menu-go-prev-diagnostic, menu-go-back, menu-go-forward with correct enabled/disabled state.
implementation_notes: Re-use, do NOT redefine, the MT-007 `DiagnosticMarker` type and the editor's existing cursor/position type; import them from src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/gutter.rs and the cursor module. Introducing a parallel marker or position struct is a defect.
Diagnostic ordering must be a total order by (line, column) ascending; if MT-007 markers are not pre-sorted, sort a borrowed copy in navigation.rs rather than mutating the gutter store.
JumpHistory is in-memory session state only — no PostgreSQL/EventLedger persistence in this MT. Cap at 50 entries, dropping the oldest.
Back/Forward truncation: pushing a new jump while history.cursor is not at the tail must truncate entries[cursor+1..] before the push (standard VS Code editor-navigation stack behavior).

Coalesce consecutive jump entries pointing at the same file within a small line tolerance so trivial caret moves do not flood the stack; only navigation jumps (goto-def, references, symbol/outline, goto-line) record entries, not ordinary typing/arrow-key movement.

Keymap extension: append to the MT-010 dispatch table; do not reorder or rewrite existing arms. Use `egui::KeyboardShortcut { modifiers: egui::Modifiers { shift, alt, .. }, key: egui::Key::F8 | egui::Key::ArrowLeft | egui::Key::ArrowRight }` consistent with sibling entries.

GO-menu items and keybindings must share ONE code path by dispatching the same CodeEditorAction through the WP-011 command bus (command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs); never duplicate the navigation logic between menu handler and key handler.
Reuse the WP-011 AccessKit id registry (accessibility/registry.rs) for the four menu-item author_ids; do not mint ad-hoc ids outside the registry.

Reuse the WP-011 native-shell modules under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/: top_menu_bar.rs for the GO menu bar surface, pane_registry.rs to resolve the focused code-editor pane, command_registry.rs + event_bus.rs as the command bus, and accessibility/registry.rs for AccessKit author_ids. Do not create parallel shell, pane, command-bus, or accessibility infrastructure.

Tests are flat under src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_*.rs (e.g. test_navigation.rs, test_jump_history.rs, test_code_editor_f8_next_diagnostic.rs); all cargo invocations target the package via `-p handshake-native`.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/operator/ProblemsView.tsx — port diagnostic ORDERING semantics only (sort by file, then line, then column, then severity) so native F8/Shift+F8 traversal order matches the Problems-list order; do not port React rendering.
binds_backend_api: NONE — pure frontend. Diagnostic markers are read from the in-process MT-007 gutter store (populated by the MT-008 LSP client); jump positions come from in-process editor cursor state. No handshake_core API call is introduced and no persistence (no PostgreSQL/EventLedger) is touched.

risks: RISK-001: Redefining `DiagnosticMarker` or the cursor position type instead of importing the MT-007/MT-001 types fragments the diagnostic model and breaks traversal-order parity with the gutter and Problems list.

RISK-002: Off-by-one in 'strictly after' / 'strictly before' comparisons causes F8 to re-select the marker the caret is already on (infinite stall) or to skip the adjacent marker. Boundary at the exact marker position must be excluded.
RISK-003: Wraparound applied to an empty marker list could panic on index 0 / last; must return None for an empty list before any indexing.

RISK-004: Forward-tail truncation bug: failing to truncate the forward entries on a new jump leaves stale forward history, so Navigate Forward jumps to an unrelated location (VS Code semantics violated).
RISK-005: Cross-file Navigate Back that opens a file via a path no longer present (file closed/deleted/renamed) panics or silently no-ops; must guard missing paths.
RISK-006: Recording every caret move (not just navigation jumps) floods the jump stack so Alt+Left steps one character at a time instead of one jump at a time.
RISK-007: Menu items and keybindings implementing the navigation twice (divergent code paths) cause menu and F8 to behave differently; parity proof would pass one and miss the other.
RISK-008: Modifying MT-010's existing keymap entries while adding the four new bindings regresses previously-shipped key handlers (Ctrl+D, Ctrl+F, Ctrl+G, fold bindings).
minimum_controls: MC-001: Unit tests import the MT-007 `DiagnosticMarker` and MT-001 cursor type directly; a compile-time assertion (or test referencing the imported path) confirms no parallel type was introduced.
MC-002: Boundary unit tests for next/prev with the cursor placed exactly on a marker position confirm the same marker is NOT returned (strict comparison).
MC-003: Empty-marker-list test asserts None for both next_diagnostic and prev_diagnostic with no panic.
MC-004: Jump-history test explicitly asserts the forward tail is truncated after a back-then-new-jump sequence.
MC-005: Navigate Back to a missing file_path returns gracefully (logs a warning, no-ops the open, leaves history cursor unchanged) — covered by a test with a bogus path.

MC-006: Only the four navigation-jump dispatch sites (goto-def, references, outline/symbol, goto-line) call JumpHistory::record; a grep proof confirms no record() call sits in ordinary caret/typing paths.

MC-007: A single dispatch function handles both menu click and keybinding (one CodeEditorAction path through the WP-011 command bus); reviewed by grep showing menu handlers dispatch actions, not inline navigation logic.
MC-008: cargo test re-runs the full MT-010 keymap test suite after this MT's edits to prove no pre-existing binding regressed.
